verdict_reasoning: LYN is a cytoplasmic Src-family tyrosine kinase canonically localized to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation. While its dominant localization is intracellular (cytoplasmic and inner-leaflet-anchored), LYN has documented surface pools and activation-induced translocation in immune cells, particularly in mast cells and B cells during degranulation and receptor signaling. The NCBI summary explicitly mentions mast cell degranulation, which involves regulated exocytosis and activation-induced PM dynamics. Src-family kinases redistribute to signaling complexes at the outer-leaflet-facing PM during immune activation; LYN reaches functional proximity to extracellular signaling events via this context-dependent mechanism. This qualifies as contextual dual localization / activation-induced display rather than baseline surface accessibility.
